negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing startup scheduler-memory hydration gate and fail-fast canonical scheduler handler. The old scheduling singleton/canonical/blocking_handlers paths are absent; the port reads Scheduler parent state directly for obligations instead of hydrating a shared scheduler memory owner.
related_but_insufficient_code: HeartbeatTracker has hydration state, but this invariant targets scheduler memory/canonical scheduling, not heartbeat liveness hydration.

rationale: The April 9 hardening candidates refine the initial hydration requirement with concurrency gating and fatal failure behavior.
statement: Before normal canonical scheduling runs from an unsynced state, exactly one caller may hydrate scheduler memory from parent-block Scheduler storage; concurrent callers observe hydration in progress, synced-block reporting remains zero until completion, hydration failures reset the gate, and the blocking canonical-state path panics rather than continuing with partial state.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start two concurrent scheduler hydration calls and assert one owns hydration while the other observes in-progress, with synced block remaining zero until success.
Force hydration failure on blocking canonical path and assert panic/fail-fast behavior.
summary: Startup scheduler-memory single-owner fail-fast hydration is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: No local SynchronousScheduler startup hydration gate exists to test.
title: Startup scheduler hydration is single-owner and fail-fast
